subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 01
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering nature, geography, science, health, and general world facts for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is a mammal?
questions.explanation: Dolphins are mammals because they breathe air, give birth to live young, and feed their babies milk.
questions.hint: Think about animals that live in the ocean but need to come to the surface to breathe air.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their characteristics.

questions.q: What is the largest continent on Earth?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent in both land area and population.
questions.hint: It is the continent where many of the world's most populous countries are located.

questions.q: What is the process called when plants use sunlight to make food?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants use sunlight to synthesize nutrients from carbon dioxide and water.
questions.hint: It is a long word that starts with 'Photo'.
questions.objective: Understand how plants live and grow.

questions.q: Which organ in the human body pumps blood?
questions.explanation: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body's circulatory system.
questions.objective: Understand the function of major human body organs.

questions.q: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars appears reddish due to iron oxide, or rust, on its surface.
questions.hint: It is the fourth planet from the Sun.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: How many days are in a leap year?
questions.explanation: A standard year has 365 days, but a leap year has one extra day in February, making it 366 days total.
questions.hint: Add one day to the number of days in a normal year.

questions.q: Which landmark is located in Paris, France?
questions.explanation: The Eiffel Tower is a famous wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris.
questions.hint: It is a very tall metal structure that is a symbol of France.

questions.q: What should you wear when riding a bicycle to stay safe?
questions.explanation: Wearing a helmet protects your head from injuries in case of a fall.
questions.hint: It protects the most important part of your body.

questions.q: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond is composed of carbon and is the hardest known natural mineral.
questions.hint: It is a very expensive gemstone often found in jewelry.
questions.objective: Learn basic facts about nature and science.

questions.q: Which gas do humans need to breathe in to stay alive?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is a gas that all humans and animals need to breathe to survive.
